# claudeg

> claudeg is an open-source Rust command-line tool that lets you run Claude Code through your existing ChatGPT subscription. It is a loopback proxy on 127.0.0.1 that translates Anthropic's Messages API into OpenAI's ChatGPT Codex API, so Claude Code talks to the local proxy instead of api.anthropic.com. You do not need a separate Anthropic API key.

## How it works

- `claudeg login` performs OAuth (PKCE) against ChatGPT and caches the token in the OS config directory.
- Every Claude Code request is translated and forwarded to ChatGPT through the local proxy.
- The Claude model in the request is auto-mapped to the appropriate ChatGPT model for your detected plan (Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise).
- Free tier is rejected because ChatGPT Free does not include API-grade access.

## Quick start

- macOS / Linux: `curl -fsSL https://claudeg.org/install.sh | sh`
- Windows / PowerShell: `iwr -useb https://claudeg.org/install.ps1 | iex`
- After install: `claudeg login`, then `claudeg` to start an interactive session.

## Commands

- `claudeg` — interactive Claude Code session routed through ChatGPT
- `claudeg "<prompt>"` — one-shot prompt
- `claudeg login` — OAuth login with ChatGPT
- `claudeg whoami` — show detected ChatGPT tier and the Claude → ChatGPT model mapping
- `claudeg logout` — forget the cached OAuth token and stop the proxy
- `claudeg setup` — register an auto-start service and pre-approve the loopback API key
- `claudeg serve [--port N]` — run the proxy in the foreground (advanced)

## Project

- Source: https://github.com/tuantong/claudeg
- License: MIT
- Language: Rust
- Platforms: macOS (aarch64, x86_64), Linux (aarch64, x86_64), Windows (x86_64)
